domains.name: PowerShell runtime
domains.objective: Capture version, profiles, execution policy, modules, aliases and path resolution.

domains.name: Terminales activas
domains.objective: Identify active shell instances, effective PATH, encoding and locale.

domains.name: Usuarios y perfiles
domains.objective: Confirm current identity, profile loading and directory permissions.

domains.name: Variables de entorno
domains.objective: Audit CODEX_*, PATH, PYTHONPATH and user/system differences.

domains.name: Entorno Codex
domains.objective: Inspect .codex, config.toml, sessions, rollouts, MCP and canonical agents.

domains.name: Configuracion del equipo
domains.objective: Record OS, hostname, architecture, timezone and baseline security policy.

domains.name: Discos y sistemas de archivos
domains.objective: List mounted volumes, free space, file systems and redirected surfaces.

domains.name: Tareas programadas
domains.objective: Inventory active tasks and isolate SDU or tooling-related jobs.

domains.name: Servicios del sistema
domains.objective: Record running services and those that affect local runtime.

domains.name: Procesos relevantes
domains.objective: Capture PowerShell, Python, Codex and suspicious or redundant processes.

checks: Capture $PSVersionTable, host, and edition.
Capture execution policy per scope.
List loaded modules, aliases, and profile files.
Confirm canonical path resolution behavior.
Enumerate pwsh, powershell, cmd, WindowsTerminal and Code processes.
Record effective PATH from the active session.
Capture culture, UI culture and output encoding.
Capture current user and security groups.
List loaded and special user profiles.
Read ACLs for USERPROFILE and .codex.
Export all process environment variables.
Compare HKCU\Environment with HKLM system environment.
Filter CODEX_* and PATH/PYTHONPATH drift.
Inspect %USERPROFILE%\.codex and its top-level nodes.
Read config.toml and related state files.
Enumerate sessions, worktrees, agents and plugins.
Record known MCP or runtime metadata if present locally.
Capture OS, build, architecture and hostname.
Capture timezone and security policy flags.
Record whether UAC is enabled and whether the session is elevated.
List volumes and filesystem types.
Measure free and total space per filesystem drive.
Record additional partitions or mounted disks.
List all scheduled tasks and current states.
Filter tasks with SDU, Codex, PowerShell, Python, Node or Git in the name.
Capture task execution metadata for suspicious or relevant tasks.
List service name, display name, status and startup mode.
Correlate services with CIM Win32_Service data.
Filter services that may affect development or runtime.
List all active processes with path and start time.
Filter PowerShell, Python, Node, Codex, Code and cmd processes.
Correlate process tree and command line for redundancy or suspicion.

risk_areas: Medium-integrity UAC token that hides the real admin boundary.
PATH shadowing between user and system scopes.
Codex cache, session or rollout drift in %USERPROFILE%\.codex.
Task Scheduler or service queries returning partial data without elevation.
Historical surfaces such as Windows.old, backups and caches polluting the scan.
Missing modules or missing pytest-style tooling in fallback runtimes.
Profiles or shells not loaded in non-interactive sessions.
Suspicious redundant processes that look like tooling but are stale.
rg is not a guaranteed dependency in this shell; prefer PowerShell-native discovery and text search.
